ui.title: How-To & Tips
ui.subtitle: Everything T'Day can do — the obvious and the hidden.

topics.title: Home and Anytime
topics.summary: Two calm home feeds you switch between from the dock.

texts: T'Day has two sibling feeds. Home holds your scheduled tasks for today and beyond. Anytime holds Floaters — tasks with no date. Switch between them from the dock at the bottom, and create a task from either one.

topics.searchKeywords: home, anytime, feed, dock, root, navigate, tabs
topics.searchBody: two calm home feeds you switch between from the dock. t'day has two sibling feeds. home holds your scheduled tasks for today and beyond. anytime holds floaters — tasks with no date. switch between them from the dock at the bottom, and create a task from either one.

topics.title: Create a task
topics.summary: Add a scheduled task or a dateless Floater in a couple of taps.

texts: Tap the create button to add a task. Give it a title and, if it has a date, a due time. Leave the date off and it becomes a Floater in your Anytime feed instead.
Start typing a date right in the title — T'Day reads it for you. See “Type the date”.

topics.searchKeywords: create, add, new, task, plus, fab, compose
topics.searchBody: add a scheduled task or a dateless floater in a couple of taps. tap the create button to add a task. give it a title and, if it has a date, a due time. leave the date off and it becomes a floater in your anytime feed instead. start typing a date right in the title — t'day reads it for you. see “type the date”.

topics.title: Type the date
topics.summary: Write the date into the title and T'Day schedules it for you.

texts: You don't need to open a date picker. Type a date phrase straight into the task title and T'Day parses it on-device — no account or network needed — highlighting the part it understood and scheduling the task.
dentist tuesday 3pm

topics.searchKeywords: natural language, date, tomorrow, next week, parse, quick, smart, nlp, on device
topics.searchBody: write the date into the title and t'day schedules it for you. you don't need to open a date picker. type a date phrase straight into the task title and t'day parses it on-device — no account or network needed — highlighting the part it understood and scheduling the task. dentist tuesday 3pm

topics.title: Calendar views
topics.summary: See your scheduled tasks by month, week, or day.

texts: Open the calendar to see scheduled tasks laid out by month, week, or day. Swipe or use the chevrons to move between periods; the top-right button jumps you back to today without changing the view.

topics.searchKeywords: calendar, month, week, day, agenda, view, dates
topics.searchBody: see your scheduled tasks by month, week, or day. open the calendar to see scheduled tasks laid out by month, week, or day. swipe or use the chevrons to move between periods; the top-right button jumps you back to today without changing the view.

topics.title: Priorities
topics.summary: Flag what matters with Low, Medium, or High.

texts: Give a task a priority to mark how much it matters. Priority shows as a small accent dot on the task and helps sort what deserves attention first.

topics.searchKeywords: priority, high, medium, low, important, urgent, flag
topics.searchBody: flag what matters with low, medium, or high. give a task a priority to mark how much it matters. priority shows as a small accent dot on the task and helps sort what deserves attention first.

topics.title: Pin a task
topics.summary: Keep an important task at the top of its list.

texts: Pin a task to hold it at the top of its feed, above the normal order. Unpin it to let it fall back into place.

topics.searchKeywords: pin, pinned, stick, top, important, keep
topics.searchBody: keep an important task at the top of its list. pin a task to hold it at the top of its feed, above the normal order. unpin it to let it fall back into place.

topics.title: Swipe actions
topics.summary: Swipe a task row for quick actions without opening it.

texts: Swipe a task row to reveal quick actions — complete it, or remove it — without opening the task at all.
A completed task shows an undo toast for a few seconds, so a mis-swipe is never final.

topics.searchKeywords: swipe, gesture, slide, complete, delete, quick, row
topics.searchBody: swipe a task row for quick actions without opening it. swipe a task row to reveal quick actions — complete it, or remove it — without opening the task at all. a completed task shows an undo toast for a few seconds, so a mis-swipe is never final.

topics.title: Long-press a task
topics.summary: Press and hold for more options and to start reordering.

texts: Press and hold a task to open its options, or to pick it up and drag it to a new position in the list.

topics.searchKeywords: long press, hold, context menu, options, reorder, drag
topics.searchBody: press and hold for more options and to start reordering. press and hold a task to open its options, or to pick it up and drag it to a new position in the list.

topics.title: Reorder by dragging
topics.summary: Drag tasks into the order you want.

texts: Your lists keep the order you give them. Drag a task up or down to arrange your day the way you think about it.

topics.searchKeywords: drag, reorder, move, sort, order, arrange
topics.searchBody: drag tasks into the order you want. your lists keep the order you give them. drag a task up or down to arrange your day the way you think about it.

topics.title: Floaters vs scheduled tasks
topics.summary: Dateless tasks live in Anytime; dated tasks live in Home.

texts: A scheduled task has a due date and shows up in Home and the calendar. A Floater has no date — it waits quietly in your Anytime feed until you're ready. Use Floaters for “someday” work that shouldn't clutter today.
Floaters never appear on the calendar — that space is only for dated work.

topics.searchKeywords: floater, anytime, unscheduled, dateless, todo, scheduled, difference
topics.searchBody: dateless tasks live in anytime; dated tasks live in home. a scheduled task has a due date and shows up in home and the calendar. a floater has no date — it waits quietly in your anytime feed until you're ready. use floaters for “someday” work that shouldn't clutter today. floaters never appear on the calendar — that space is only for dated work.

topics.title: Promote & float
topics.summary: Schedule a floater into a real task, or let a stale task float.

texts: When a floater is ready to become real work, use its Schedule action: pick a time and it moves into Home as a dated task. The other direction works too — an overdue task you keep postponing can quietly float: demote it and it returns to Anytime with no date, keeping its title, notes and priority.
Recurring tasks can't float — end the series first if you want it dateless.

topics.searchKeywords: promote, schedule floater, demote, let it float, convert, anytime, move, overdue
topics.searchBody: schedule a floater into a real task, or let a stale task float. when a floater is ready to become real work, use its schedule action: pick a time and it moves into home as a dated task. the other direction works too — an overdue task you keep postponing can quietly float: demote it and it returns to anytime with no date, keeping its title, notes and priority. recurring tasks can't float — end the series first if you want it dateless.

topics.title: Quick Defer
topics.summary: One tap moves a task to later today, tonight, tomorrow or next week.

texts: When a task needs to move but not far, skip the date picker: the Defer action offers Later today (+3 hours), Tonight (19:00), Tomorrow morning (09:00) and Next week (Monday 09:00) as one-tap choices. It's the same change the edit sheet would make — just faster.
Recurring tasks defer per-occurrence from the edit sheet, so the one-tap menu stays out of their way.

topics.searchKeywords: defer, postpone, snooze task, later, tonight, tomorrow, next week, reschedule
topics.searchBody: one tap moves a task to later today, tonight, tomorrow or next week. when a task needs to move but not far, skip the date picker: the defer action offers later today (+3 hours), tonight (19:00), tomorrow morning (09:00) and next week (monday 09:00) as one-tap choices. it's the same change the edit sheet would make — just faster. recurring tasks defer per-occurrence from the edit sheet, so the one-tap menu stays out of their way.

topics.title: Day Done
topics.summary: Finish everything for today and the Today screen quietly celebrates.

texts: When every task scheduled for today is completed — not merely absent — the Today screen swaps its "No tasks" message for a calm Day Done state: a soft double-check, the words "All done for today" and the date. No streaks, no scores; just the small payoff of an actually finished day.

topics.searchKeywords: day done, all done, finished, completed everything, empty today, celebration
topics.searchBody: finish everything for today and the today screen quietly celebrates. when every task scheduled for today is completed — not merely absent — the today screen swaps its "no tasks" message for a calm day done state: a soft double-check, the words "all done for today" and the date. no streaks, no scores; just the small payoff of an actually finished day.

topics.title: Floater lists
topics.summary: Group your Anytime tasks into their own lists.

texts: Organize Floaters into separate lists — groceries, packing, ideas — each with its own name and color, and its own completed history.

topics.searchKeywords: floater list, anytime, group, list, category, groceries, packing
topics.searchBody: group your anytime tasks into their own lists. organize floaters into separate lists — groceries, packing, ideas — each with its own name and color, and its own completed history.

topics.title: Lists for scheduled tasks
topics.summary: Sort dated tasks into named, colored lists.

texts: Give scheduled tasks a list so related work stays together. Lists carry a name and color and can be filtered.

topics.searchKeywords: list, project, group, color, scheduled, category
topics.searchBody: sort dated tasks into named, colored lists. give scheduled tasks a list so related work stays together. lists carry a name and color and can be filtered.

topics.title: Completed history
topics.summary: Everything you finished, kept for you to look back on.

texts: Finished tasks aren't discarded — they move to your completed history, grouped by day, so you can see what you actually got done.

topics.searchKeywords: completed, done, history, finished, archive, past
topics.searchBody: everything you finished, kept for you to look back on. finished tasks aren't discarded — they move to your completed history, grouped by day, so you can see what you actually got done.

topics.title: Overdue
topics.summary: A dedicated place for tasks whose time has passed.

texts: Tasks whose due time has passed collect in the Overdue view so nothing slips silently. Reschedule them when you're ready.

topics.searchKeywords: overdue, late, past due, missed, carried over, backlog
topics.searchBody: a dedicated place for tasks whose time has passed. tasks whose due time has passed collect in the overdue view so nothing slips silently. reschedule them when you're ready.

topics.title: Morning Sweep
topics.summary: Triage carried-over tasks one card at a time.

texts: Getting behind stops hurting: the Sweep button on the Overdue screen walks your carried-over tasks one card at a time — Today, Tomorrow, Pick a date, Make it a floater, or Let it go. In a hurry? Sweep all to today moves everything at once, with a single undo.
Recurring tasks stay out of the sweep; their occurrences reschedule from the edit sheet so the series stays intact.

topics.searchKeywords: sweep, overdue, triage, carried over, catch up, backlog, morning
topics.searchBody: triage carried-over tasks one card at a time. getting behind stops hurting: the sweep button on the overdue screen walks your carried-over tasks one card at a time — today, tomorrow, pick a date, make it a floater, or let it go. in a hurry? sweep all to today moves everything at once, with a single undo. recurring tasks stay out of the sweep; their occurrences reschedule from the edit sheet so the series stays intact.

topics.title: Search your tasks
topics.summary: Find any task by title, note, or list name.

texts: Use the search field to filter your tasks instantly by title, description, or list name — no need to scroll.

topics.searchKeywords: search, find, filter, lookup, query
topics.searchBody: find any task by title, note, or list name. use the search field to filter your tasks instantly by title, description, or list name — no need to scroll.

topics.title: Repeating tasks
topics.summary: Make a task repeat daily, weekly, and more.

texts: Set a task to repeat and it comes back on its own schedule. Pick from the presets — every day, weekday, week, or month — and completing one occurrence leaves the rest of the series intact.
Water the plants · every week

topics.searchKeywords: repeat, recurring, recurrence, every day, weekly, monthly, rrule, schedule
topics.searchBody: make a task repeat daily, weekly, and more. set a task to repeat and it comes back on its own schedule. pick from the presets — every day, weekday, week, or month — and completing one occurrence leaves the rest of the series intact. water the plants · every week

topics.title: Reminders
topics.summary: Get a notification before a task is due.

texts: Add a reminder to a scheduled task and your device notifies you at the right time. Reminders are scheduled on your device, so they work even in Local Mode.
Reminder lead-time options vary slightly by task — pick the one that gives you enough warning.

topics.searchKeywords: reminder, notification, alert, alarm, notify, before
topics.searchBody: get a notification before a task is due. add a reminder to a scheduled task and your device notifies you at the right time. reminders are scheduled on your device, so they work even in local mode. reminder lead-time options vary slightly by task — pick the one that gives you enough warning.

topics.title: Snooze a reminder
topics.summary: Reminder notifications carry Snooze 1h and Tonight actions.

texts: When a reminder fires at a bad moment, you don't have to open the app: Snooze 1h re-delivers the same reminder an hour later, and Tonight moves the task itself to 19:00 today. Tonight works for one-off tasks — recurring occurrences reschedule from the app so their series stays intact.
Snoozing only delays the notification; the task keeps its original due time until you move it.

topics.searchKeywords: snooze, reminder, notification, later, tonight, postpone alert
topics.searchBody: reminder notifications carry snooze 1h and tonight actions. when a reminder fires at a bad moment, you don't have to open the app: snooze 1h re-delivers the same reminder an hour later, and tonight moves the task itself to 19:00 today. tonight works for one-off tasks — recurring occurrences reschedule from the app so their series stays intact. snoozing only delays the notification; the task keeps its original due time until you move it.

topics.title: Day Ahead digest
topics.summary: One quiet morning notification with today's counts.

texts: Opt in from Settings and pick an hour: each morning one notification tells you how the day looks — "4 tasks today, 2 carried over" — and opens Today, or jumps straight into Morning Sweep when something is carried over. Fully on-device: nothing leaves your phone.
The digest stays silent on task-free days — no news is good news.

topics.searchKeywords: day ahead, digest, morning summary, daily notification, overview
topics.searchBody: one quiet morning notification with today's counts. opt in from settings and pick an hour: each morning one notification tells you how the day looks — "4 tasks today, 2 carried over" — and opens today, or jumps straight into morning sweep when something is carried over. fully on-device: nothing leaves your phone. the digest stays silent on task-free days — no news is good news.

topics.title: Home-screen widget
topics.summary: See today's tasks without opening the app.

texts: Add the T'Day widget to your home screen to glance at today's tasks. Tapping the widget opens the app at the right place.

topics.searchKeywords: widget, home screen, glance, today, lock screen
topics.searchBody: see today's tasks without opening the app. add the t'day widget to your home screen to glance at today's tasks. tapping the widget opens the app at the right place.

topics.title: Quick-add from the widget
topics.summary: Start a new task straight from the widget.

texts: The widget has a create button, so you can capture a task from your home screen without first opening the app — and it works in Local Mode too.

topics.searchKeywords: widget, quick add, create, shortcut, home screen
topics.searchBody: start a new task straight from the widget. the widget has a create button, so you can capture a task from your home screen without first opening the app — and it works in local mode too.

topics.title: Complete from the widget
topics.summary: Check off tasks right on the home-screen widget.

texts: The Today and Floater widgets take taps now: hit the ring at the start of any row to complete that task without opening the app. The row disappears immediately and the completion syncs like any other — taps made offline queue up and apply once you're back.
Mis-tapped? Open the Completed history in the app and restore the task from there.

topics.searchKeywords: widget, complete, check off, home screen, interactive, tap
topics.searchBody: check off tasks right on the home-screen widget. the today and floater widgets take taps now: hit the ring at the start of any row to complete that task without opening the app. the row disappears immediately and the completion syncs like any other — taps made offline queue up and apply once you're back. mis-tapped? open the completed history in the app and restore the task from there.

topics.title: Share into T'Day
topics.summary: Send text from any app straight into a new task.

texts: Found something in another app? Open the system share sheet and pick T'Day. Shared text lands in a new-task form — a shared page keeps its title as the task name with the link tucked into notes. On Android and the web app the form opens on the spot; on iOS the capture is saved instantly and the prefilled form greets you next time you open the app.
Share text that contains a date phrase and the parser sets the due date for you.

topics.searchKeywords: share, share sheet, send to, capture, import, from another app
topics.searchBody: send text from any app straight into a new task. found something in another app? open the system share sheet and pick t'day. shared text lands in a new-task form — a shared page keeps its title as the task name with the link tucked into notes. on android and the web app the form opens on the spot; on ios the capture is saved instantly and the prefilled form greets you next time you open the app. share text that contains a date phrase and the parser sets the due date for you.

topics.title: T'Day on CarPlay
topics.summary: Glance at and capture tasks safely while you drive.

texts: On CarPlay, T'Day shows a focused, glanceable list built for the car, so you can check what's next or add a task by voice without reaching for your phone.

topics.searchKeywords: carplay, car, driving, voice, apple, hands free
topics.searchBody: glance at and capture tasks safely while you drive. on carplay, t'day shows a focused, glanceable list built for the car, so you can check what's next or add a task by voice without reaching for your phone.

topics.title: Local Mode
topics.summary: Use T'Day fully offline, with no server or account.

texts: Local Mode keeps everything on your device — no login, no server, no upload. It's a first-class way to use T'Day privately, and your local data is never sent anywhere on its own.
Server-only features are shown here with a “Server mode” badge so you know what changes if you sign in.

topics.searchKeywords: local mode, offline, private, no account, no server, on device
topics.searchBody: use t'day fully offline, with no server or account. local mode keeps everything on your device — no login, no server, no upload. it's a first-class way to use t'day privately, and your local data is never sent anywhere on its own. server-only features are shown here with a “server mode” badge so you know what changes if you sign in.

topics.title: Server Mode
topics.summary: Sync across devices through your own private server.

texts: Sign in to your self-hosted server to sync tasks across devices in real time. Writes happen instantly on your device and replay to the server, so the app stays fast even on a shaky connection.

topics.searchKeywords: server mode, sync, self hosted, account, login, devices, realtime
topics.searchBody: sync across devices through your own private server. sign in to your self-hosted server to sync tasks across devices in real time. writes happen instantly on your device and replay to the server, so the app stays fast even on a shaky connection.

topics.title: Offline sync
topics.summary: Keep working offline; changes replay when you reconnect.

texts: In Server Mode your changes are saved locally first and queued when you're offline. As soon as you reconnect, T'Day replays them to the server in order.

topics.searchKeywords: offline, sync, pending, queue, replay, connection, conflict
topics.searchBody: keep working offline; changes replay when you reconnect. in server mode your changes are saved locally first and queued when you're offline. as soon as you reconnect, t'day replays them to the server in order.

topics.title: Task summaries
topics.summary: A short, calm read on your day — with optional local AI.

texts: T'Day can summarize your day in a sentence or two. With a local Ollama model it reads naturally; without one it falls back to a warm, deterministic recap — either way nothing leaves your server.
The summary never depends on a cloud AI service — it's local or nothing.

topics.searchKeywords: summary, ai, ollama, overview, digest, local, private
topics.searchBody: a short, calm read on your day — with optional local ai. t'day can summarize your day in a sentence or two. with a local ollama model it reads naturally; without one it falls back to a warm, deterministic recap — either way nothing leaves your server. the summary never depends on a cloud ai service — it's local or nothing.
